untranslatable_reason: Signal requires a directional forecaster model with hit rate accuracy >=52%, p-value <=0.10 statistical significance testing, and minimum 100 test samples. These are meta-strategy validation metrics requiring external model evaluation, not standard price/indicator conditions. The signal is about when to *activate* a strategy based on ML model performance, not price action itself. Standard indicators cannot replicate directional forecast accuracy, p-value computation, or sample size validation without access to the forecasting model's predictions and statistical test results.
translation_notes: This signal is a meta-rule for strategy activation based on ML model backtesting performance rather than market conditions. It requires: (1) a trained directional forecasting model, (2) out-of-sample validation with >=100 samples, (3) statistical significance testing (p-value), and (4) MAE measurement. These cannot be expressed as standard technical indicator rules. Implementation requires: external model validation framework, hit rate calculation on rolling windows, p-value computation via binomial test, and MAE tracking—all outside the scope of indicator-based entry/exit logic.
